description: Wallet is a non-interactive identity row composed of a decorative jazzicon, an optional display name, a truncated on-chain address, and a copy-to-clipboard button. The avatar is marked aria-hidden because it encodes no information beyond the address itself, which is already rendered as text.

keyboard.action: Moves focus to the copy button (the only interactive element).
Activates the copy button and copies the full address to the clipboard.

focus.notes: Only the copy button is focusable. The row itself is not a tab stop, so embedding Wallet inside a list, menu, or button is safe.

announcements: The copy button's aria-label toggles between "Copy address" and "Address copied" so a screen reader announces the success of the copy action.
screenReader.notes: Use the `name` prop when possible; screen readers will read the name, then the address, which matches the visual order. When only the address is shown, consider wrapping the component in a region with a heading so the context (e.g. "Connected wallet") is clear.

contrast.text: Address text uses text-muted-foreground; meets WCAG AA over bg-card in the default theme
contrast.ui: Copy button icon meets WCAG AA via the Button ghost variant

motion.reducedMotion: No animations beyond the 1.5s copy-confirmation icon swap

consumerResponsibilities: Provide a real wallet address; the jazzicon seed is derived from its hex bytes, so consistent capitalization keeps the avatar stable across renders.
When displaying multiple wallets in a list, wrap them in a <ul>/<li> structure so assistive tech can report the count and position.
The full address is exposed via the `title` attribute on WalletAddress; for users who can't hover, pair Wallet with a visible "Show full address" affordance if the full value matters.
